data.title: Analyst says traders predicting Q4 price peak for Bitcoin deemed to misunderstand statistics, with cycle high unlikely.
data.description: According to Bitcoin analyst PlanC, there is little reason to expect Bitcoin to reach a peak in its cycle this year, citing it as mainly a result of a "psychological, self-fulfilling prophecy."

data.title: Planet Labs (NYSE: PL) to release Q2 earnings with analysts predicting loss at 4 cents/share, revenue at $65.93 million. Shares rose 0.3% to $6.46.
data.description: Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on Monday, Sept. 8. Analysts are predicting a quarterly loss of 4 cents per share for the San Francisco-based company, an improvement from the 6-cent loss reported a year ago. Revenue for this quarter is expected to hit $65.93 million, up from the $61.09 million reported in the same period last year. The company recently secured major contract wins with global defense and intelligence agencies, including a €240 million deal funded by Germany. This agreement includes a multi-year, eight-figure annual renewal for data and AI services. Planet Labs PBC's shares closed at $6.46 on Thursday, marking a 0.3% increase. For more details on the latest analyst insights, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Poor job numbers indicate a likely interest rate cut at Fed's upcoming meeting, following weak August data.
data.description: It is highly likely that there will be a decrease in interest rates at the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting this month following the release of August's labor market figures.

data.title: Teamsters at University of Minnesota reject employer's final offer by 82%. 1,400 workers impacted.
data.description: Employees belonging to Teamsters Local 320 at the University of Minnesota have declined their employer's final offer by a significant margin of 82%. The 1,400 Teamsters who are employed in roles such as custodial and maintenance services have adamantly opposed the proposal put forth by the university.

data.title: Malaysia's construction equipment market set to grow at 5.83% CAGR from 2024-2030. Leading companies include Caterpillar, Komatsu, Volvo CE, Hitachi Construction Machinery, and SANY.
data.description: A report on the Malaysia Construction Equipment Market forecasts a growth rate of 5.83% from 2024 to 2030. Key players in the market include Caterpillar, Komatsu, Volvo CE, Hitachi Construction Machinery, and SANY, with a significant market share and diverse equipment portfolios. Other notable companies in the sector are Tadano, Terex, XCMG, Kubota, and Zoomli. This data was added to the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com.

data.title: Colabor Group Inc. reaches forbearance agreements with principal lenders, including TD Bank and BMO, and Investissement Québec.
data.description: Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, on September 5, 2025 - Colabor Group Inc. (TSX: GCL) has announced the signing of a forbearance agreement with its main lenders, namely The Toronto-Dominion Bank, Bank of Montreal, and The Bank of Nova Scotia. This agreement applies to the amended and restated senior first-ranking secured credit facility. Additionally, separate forbearance agreements have been made with Investissement Québec regarding the subordinated and highly subordinated credit facilities. These agreements, collectively referred to as the "Forbearance Agreements," aim to address financial issues impacting the company.

data.title: Singapore Festival 2025 to celebrate culture, community, and 60 years of nationhood in a first-of-its-kind event.
data.description: A unique event, the Singapore Festival 2025, is set to take place in celebration of culture, community, and the nation's 60th anniversary. This inaugural festival will be held in San Mateo, California, featuring various activities and showcases highlighting Singaporean heritage and achievements. Join us for this historic occasion, bringing together individuals to commemorate a significant milestone in Singapore's history.

data.title: Regulators suggest making US financial markets open 24/7 to align with crypto. They propose policies to enhance DeFi.
data.description: Government agencies have introduced new regulations aimed at speeding up the development of forecasting markets, perpetual markets, and other DeFi protocols within the United States. These regulations are designed to facilitate the growth and expansion of these innovative financial technologies within the country's borders.

data.title: SOL Strategies gains Nasdaq approval with listing set for Sept. 9, transitioning from OTC to US market.
data.description: A Canadian company has taken steps towards entering the US market by securing a listing on the Nasdaq on September 9th. This move shifted its trading activities from the over-the-counter venture market to the Nasdaq platform.

data.title: Dogwifhat (WIF) trading at $0.81 shows 0.37% uptick. RSI neutral, MACD bullish, hinting mixed signals.
data.description: The WIF price is currently trading at $0.81, showing a slight 0.37% increase for the day. A technical analysis by dogwifhat indicates conflicting momentum signals, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) at a neutral level and a bullish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) crossover starting to emerge.

data.title: Braze Inc surpasses Q2 expectations with $180.1M revenue, $16.9M net income. FY2026 EPS forecast raised to $717M - $720M. Analysts revise outlook on Braze.
data.description: Braze Inc (NYSE: BRZE) recently released its second-quarter results, surpassing expectations and revising its FY2026 EPS and sales forecast. In Q2 2026, the company reported revenue of $180.1 million, a 23.8% increase from $145.5 million in the previous year, exceeding the estimated $170.1 million. Their non-GAAP net income reached $16.9 million, or 15 cents per diluted share, significantly higher than the estimated 3 cents and up from $9.1 million, or 9 cents per diluted share, in the same period last year. For Q3, Braze anticipates revenue between $183.5 million and $184.5 million, surpassing the $178.6 million estimate, with non-GAAP net income expected to be between $6.5 million to $7.5 million, equivalent to 6 cents to 7 cents per diluted share, above the projected 2 cents. Additionally, for the fiscal year ending January 31, 2026, the company has raised its outlook, now anticipating revenue ranging from $717.0 million to $720.0 million.

data.title: Investor rights law firm Halper Sadeh LLC investigates potential violations by Air Lease Corp (NYSE: AL) and Phreesia Inc (NYSE: PHLT), Sept. 5, 2025.
data.description: On September 5, 2025, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in protecting investor rights, announced their investigation into several companies for potential breaches of federal securities laws and fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firms under scrutiny include Air Lease Corporation (NYSE: AL). The investigation aims to uncover any violations or misconduct in connection to the sale of the company. This development marks a significant step in ensuring transparency and accountability in the financial sector. Investors and stakeholders are encouraged to stay informed as the situation unfolds to safeguard their interests.

data.title: Approximately 500 South Koreans were caught in an immigration raid at a Georgia Hyundai plant, raising diplomatic concerns in Seoul.
data.description: Around 500 workers were caught at a construction site belonging to a South Korean battery manufacturer, as per officials. The incident raised diplomatic worries in Seoul.

data.title: Christina Lake Cannabis extends debenture worth $710,000 to mature on September 7, 2026, up from September 7, 2022.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on September 5, 2025, Christina Lake Cannabis Corp. (CLC) disclosed amendments to the terms of convertible debentures worth $710,000. The holders of the debentures, issued on September 7, 2022, have agreed to extend the maturity date to September 7, 2026. The company operates under the tickers CSE: CLC, OTCQB: CLCFF, and FRANKFURT: CLB. This update follows a previous press release from August 22, 2025.

data.title: Douglas Emmett, Inc. (DEI) declares $0.19 quarterly dividend per share ($0.76 annualized) on NYSE. Paid Oct 15 to shareholders at record on Sep 30, 2025.
data.description: In Santa Monica, California, Douglas Emmett, Inc. (NYSE: DEI), a real estate investment trust (REIT), disclosed today that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.19 per share of common stock, totaling $0.76 annually. This dividend is scheduled to be disbursed on October 15, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of September 30, 2025. Douglas Emmett, Inc. (DEI) is a fully integrated, self-administered, and self-managed REIT.

data.title: Tineco showcases smart floor care solutions at IFA with wet-dry vacuums and cordless cleaners, redefining smart cleaning.
data.description: At a technology event in Berlin, leading household technology manufacturer Tineco introduced its latest product lineup, showcasing an array of new solutions aimed at addressing common household issues. The focus of the unveiling was on wet-dry vacuums and cordless vacuum cleaners. These products are equipped with state-of-the-art sensors, advanced self-cleaning capabilities, and intelligent controls, marking a significant advancement in the realm of smart cleaning. Tineco's innovative features cater to user requirements, ensuring exceptional functionality and convenience.

data.title: Thumzup to acquire DogeHash with 3,500 Dogecoin mining rigs. Expected revenue $22.7-$51.6M annually. Shift to crypto mining.
data.description: Thumzup Media Corporation (NASDAQ: TZUP) announced plans to acquire DogeHash Technologies, aiming to take control of 3,500 Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) mining rigs. This shift from advertising technology to cryptocurrency mining was revealed in a shareholder letter. DogeHash currently operates 2,500 mining rigs, with an additional 1,000 rigs expected to be delivered this year. The acquisition is pending shareholder approval. Thumzup anticipates that with a total of 3,500 rigs, annual revenue could range from $22.7 million to $51.6 million. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
